sanskrit: अव्यक्तोऽक्षर इत्युक्तस्तमाहुः परमां गतिम् । यं प्राप्य न निवर्तन्ते तद्धाम परमं मम ॥
hindiTranslation: उसीको अव्यक्त और अक्षर कहा गया है और उसीको परमगति कहा गया है तथा जिसको प्राप्त होनेपर जीव फिर लौटकर नहीं आते, वह मेरा परमधाम है ॥
englishTranslation: That is called the unmanifest and the imperishable. It is the highest goal. Having reached it, one does not return. It is my supreme abode.
essence: The highest arrival is beyond return.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: The highest reality is beyond change and form. Whoever reaches it does not come back to the cycle of repeated return.
simpleInsight: There is relief in knowing that the deepest destination is permanent, not temporary.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna is still frozen while Krishna explains what lies beyond all changing worlds. After describing the unmanifest and imperishable, Krishna names it his supreme abode: the final destination from which no one returns.
modernRelevance: You finally choose the harder right over the easier wrong, and something in you knows there is no going back. This verse speaks to that irreversible threshold.
translationLiteral: अव्यक्तः (unmanifest) / अक्षरः (imperishable) / इति (thus) / उक्तः (called) / तम् (that) / आहुः (they call) / परमाम् (highest) / गतिम् (goal) / यम् (which) / प्राप्य (having attained) / न (not) / निवर्तन्ते (return) / तत् (that) / धाम (abode) / परमम् (supreme) / मम (my)
insight: It shifts the mind from temporary achievement to final release from return.

quoteVariants.text: Reach the unchanging. Return ends there.
What is reached here never sends you back.
Reach the imperishable, and return ends.
